checklist.description: A preview endpoint exists that assembles a custom pack from a caller-supplied entry list and returns the result without creating any database record.
checklist.example: POST /diaries/:id/packs/preview calls materializeCustomPack with persist=false and returns the result directly without touching contextPackRepository.createPack.
checklist.name: preview_endpoint_no_persist

checklist.description: A create endpoint exists that assembles and persists a custom pack, returning HTTP 201 with the same result shape as the preview endpoint.
checklist.example: POST /diaries/:id/packs calls materializeCustomPack with persist=true and replies with reply.code(201).send(pack).
checklist.name: create_endpoint_persists

checklist.description: When a token budget is supplied and the total token count exceeds it, lower-ranked entries are compressed progressively (full → summary → keywords) and dropped if the budget is still exceeded, before touching higher-ranked entries.
checklist.example: fitCustomPackEntries iterates selectedEntries in reverse rank order across three passes (full→summary, summary→keywords, drop) until totalTokens fits the budget.
checklist.name: token_budget_compression

checklist.description: The response includes a deterministic content identifier for the custom pack, computed from the pack's inputs.
checklist.example: computePackCid({ diaryId, packType: 'custom', params, entries }) is called and the result is returned as packCid in the response.
checklist.name: pack_cid_computed

checklist.description: Both preview and create responses include compilation statistics: total tokens, number of entries included and compressed, compression ratio, budget utilization, and elapsed time.
checklist.example: fitCustomPackEntries returns a stats object with totalTokens, entriesIncluded, entriesCompressed, compressionRatio, budgetUtilization, elapsedMs; the stats are returned as compileStats in the response.
checklist.name: compile_stats_returned

checklist.description: Submitting a pack selection with duplicate entry IDs results in a validation error response, not a silent merge or server error.
checklist.example: normalizeSelection throws createProblem('validation-failed', ...) when a duplicate entryId is detected.
checklist.name: validation_duplicate_entry_ids

checklist.description: Entries that are not found in the target diary produce a validation error that identifies the missing entry IDs.
checklist.example: loadSelectedEntries compares loaded rows to requested entries and throws createProblem('validation-failed', `Entries not found in diary ${diaryId}: ${missingIds.join(', ')}`).
checklist.name: validation_missing_entries

checklist.description: The implementation accesses the diary using the same service abstraction as other diary-scoped routes, including auth-aware access control.
checklist.example: fastify.diaryService.findDiary(diaryId, identityId) is called with a try/catch that delegates to translateFindDiaryError for diary-level errors (not-found, forbidden, internal).
checklist.name: uses_existing_diary_service_pattern

checklist.description: The token budget fitting logic uses the existing compression utilities from the codebase rather than re-implementing them.
checklist.example: compress(entry, level) and estimateTokens(content) from @moltnet/context-distill are used to build and measure DistillEntry instances.
checklist.name: uses_existing_compression_library

checklist.description: New response schemas for the custom pack result are added to the shared schema registry so they can be referenced via $ref in OpenAPI output.
checklist.example: CustomPackResultSchema and CustomPackEntryResultSchema are added to the sharedSchemas array in apps/rest-api/src/schemas.ts.
checklist.name: response_schemas_registered

checklist.description: When a custom pack is persisted, the authorization system is updated with the correct parent relationship between the pack and its diary.
checklist.example: After contextPackRepository.createPack, fastify.relationshipWriter.grantPackParent(pack.id, diary.id) is called; on failure, the pack is deleted and an internal error is returned.
checklist.name: persisted_pack_keto_grant

checklist.description: When a custom pack is persisted, its entries (including compression level, token counts, rank, and CID snapshot) are recorded in the pack entry repository.
checklist.example: contextPackRepository.addEntries is called inside the transaction with entryCidSnapshot, compressionLevel, originalTokens, packedTokens, and rank fields for each entry.
checklist.name: persisted_pack_includes_entries

checklist.description: A packs_preview MCP tool is registered and callable by agents, mapping snake_case MCP inputs to the REST API's camelCase request body.
checklist.example: fastify.mcpAddTool({ name: 'packs_preview', inputSchema: PackPreviewSchema }, ...) is registered in registerPackTools; handler maps entry_id→entryId, token_budget→tokenBudget.
checklist.name: mcp_preview_tool_registered

checklist.description: A packs_create MCP tool is registered and callable by agents, mapping snake_case MCP inputs to the REST API's camelCase request body.
checklist.example: fastify.mcpAddTool({ name: 'packs_create', inputSchema: PackCreateSchema }, ...) is registered in registerPackTools; handler maps entry_id→entryId, token_budget→tokenBudget.
checklist.name: mcp_create_tool_registered

checklist.description: MCP tool error responses surface the specific error detail from the API (e.g. the detail or message field from ProblemDetails) rather than a hardcoded generic string.
checklist.example: extractApiErrorMessage(error, fallback) is used in MCP handlers for pack operations, returning ProblemDetails.detail or .message when available.
checklist.name: mcp_error_messages_descriptive

checklist.description: Persisted custom packs respect the pinned flag: pinned packs have no expiry, unpinned packs expire after a set duration consistent with the existing compile flow.
checklist.example: expiresAt is set to null when pinned=true, otherwise set to 7 days from createdAt.
checklist.name: pinning_expiry_behavior

checklist.description: Persisted custom packs are stored with a distinct pack type that identifies them as caller-assembled rather than auto-compiled.
checklist.example: contextPackRepository.createPack is called with packType: 'custom'; the payload includes packType: 'custom'.
checklist.name: pack_type_custom

context: Evaluates adding custom pack preview/create endpoints to the REST API and corresponding MCP tools, allowing callers to supply explicit entry selections with caller-defined rankings instead of relying on the automated compile workflow.
